memory.recent_lessons_md:
### 2026-06-08 Pre-Market

**Iran-Israel conflict creates asymmetric sector opportunities:** Same macro event (oil +4.5%) is bearish for most tech (inflation = rate hike fears) but bullish for PLTR (defense AI spending), GLD (safe haven), and energy. This divergence is alpha-generating — know which names benefit from each catalyst vector.

**S&P 500 inclusion = structural demand catalyst:** MRVL +9% on index announcement is not a pump-and-dump. Index funds MUST buy $2-4B in MRVL shares to match S&P weighting. This buying continues for weeks as funds build positions. Entry at $288.50 (only $1.45 above premarket price) captures the structural demand without chasing the initial spike.

**28th consecutive API block: operator execution is the only path.** The cloud runner's network policy does not include `paper-api.alpaca.markets`. Until Anthropic adds it to the allowlist, every order requires operator manual execution at https://app.alpaca.markets. The strategy is sound; the execution layer is broken. Every deployment failure is documented with full order details to minimize friction for manual execution.

**Prior lesson (June 4): GLD price estimates can be materially wrong.** Always note source and time of estimates. Today GLD is estimated at $415-425 based on Iran-Israel gold bid — this is an estimate, not confirmed.
